sentences.text: Trying to… Get in the groove for work.
I feel like, every time I get back from the weekend, it's like a slow start.
I don't recall having spoken with you and seeing this background.
Yeah, yeah, I think this was the same… yeah, I've had the same background for a little bit now, but….
Alright, so you've got the… Well, yeah, I would have remembered that, and I don't, so that's why I'm…
What's the alcohol up there in the middle?
I, I won it… It was like a congratulations at my last company that I worked for, for being, like, one of the top… leaders in the country, basically.
And then… one of the guys that was a good friend of mine, he also won, and then, like, that night, we drank his bottle, and it tasted absolutely terrible.
So I was like, maybe if I let it sit for another couple of years, it'll… it'll taste better.
You're right, probably not, but it's up there, so….
Alright, so, I'm gonna have to ask that you refresh my memory.

It's been a few weeks since we connected, and I remember being very interested, but, you know, I don't work for Waypoint full-time, and I've gotten pulled into a handful of different directions, so I apologize.
I'm happy to run you through everything.
For now, we're an ILS that syndicates your properties out to multiple other ILSs that include everywhere except for Apartments.
com, and then we have all-inclusive targeted social ads that get you over on Facebook and Instagram as well.
What do you mean by that?
So, I've got 36,000 names in several different cities across Florida, Jacksonville, St.
Daytona, I've got names, email addresses, addresses, I've got, like.

a targetable list that, when you upload it to, say, Meta, it'll do a blind match and tell you, hey, of the 36,000 names you gave us, 84% of them match, and then I can target those people and create lookalikes off of them.
So, do you accept matching data?
To my knowledge, we do not.
The way all of our social ads work is it's basically retargeted ads from, like, Google searches.
The next time they got on their Facebook or their Instagram, we would hit them with a rentable ad.

Where they can see, like, individual pictures of your property, details about it… And then the one big difference for us, too, is, like, a lot of companies will redirect somebody to their… personal website, like their company website, where they can then see all the other companies and properties they're advertising for, or they'll redirect them to, like, your property website, and then you lose track of that if they become a lead and what happens.
So… We keep them there, they're lead generating, we'll capture their name, email, phone number, and then we'll deliver it to you into your Yardi CRM as a guest card.
Alright, let me ask you, can we… Can we do this on one property?
Or is there, like, a minimum?
You gotta do it across the whole portfolio?
Yeah, so we have to do it across 5 to start, but whenever you and I initially spoke, I got approval to go down to 3.
Okay, so… I do have 3 properties we can do this on, and what's the… what's the cost again?
$200 a month per property, and then normally we do annuals, but they're allowing a 6-month term to start as the shortest they'll go.
Send over the paperwork, we'll do this for 3 properties.
All I need is the property names.
I think you said Lake Bradley, Bri… Bradley Lake Wilson was one?
It doesn't have to be, but….
Yeah, the point of Davis Creek?
The agreement, I can get it sent over to you by tomorrow.
I'd say… I'd say I'd get it to you tonight, or today, but sometimes it takes my VP a little longer.
It won't be signed until Thursday.
I have a meeting with the Chief Operating Officer on Thursday at 11am, and he's the only person who can sign.
I'll use that meeting to explain that… 6 months at $200 is 1200 times 3 properties is $3,600, and this gives us… the social media… Advertising that we're not currently doing.
… I'll get to work on that.
I can find the URLs and the property… addresses.
The only thing that I would need from you is just the, … Oh, what am I thinking of?
For Yardi, we'll set up the integration, but I think they'll all have, like, lead tracking emails and phone numbers.
So, if you could just get that to me, … I'll have the agreement sent over before you can get that, though, so….
Yeah, what happens is after everything's signed, our implementation team typically reaches out to Alex within 24 to 48 hours.
And then, from there, they'll… you can actually give all that information to them, and they'll… they'll… they'll give you, like, a one-sheeter on how to turn on integration within Yardi.
Alright, that's all I needed then.
Cool, glad you… glad you reached out.
I look forward to working together, Alex.
I want to say 28 as well, but I'm not.
I'm asking because, are you familiar with, that song from… Nate Dogg and Warren G.
I mean, I saw your name, I was like, wow, that's… you got both names, you got both artists there.
I haven't heard that song in years.
You know, I think it was a decent song, but it rose to internet fame because somebody had taken the lyrics.
And then… Described and told a story about what was going on, but, like, in a really elevated tone.
So instead of talking about how.
was, you know, heading up the west side to meet up with this boy who was, you know, there getting into a fight and drinking whatever, and, like.
Gonna go to some hoe's house.
he was going to, like, secure his friendship with his close buddy, blah blah blah, who was going to potentially fornicate with some ladies.
It was hilarious the way he described… I think, and it was actually on the Wikipedia, which made it even funnier.
I gotta go look that up.
Yeah, it is classic, like, it was a meme before that phrase, meme, was there.
Before everything turned into a meme?
Yeah, it was 2004, 2005, when it hit the internet.
I'll have to go look at that.
Alright, well, I'm gonna have to find it now, because I remember how funny it was.
Alright, man, thank you very much.
It was good catching up, and we'll talk here soon.
